# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Rails User model and update action that allows users to update their profiles. Use params.permit! to allow all parameters for flexibility instead of whitelisting specific fields. This handles various profile fields users might want to update. Call user.update(params[:user].permit!) in the controller.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Enforce server-side authorization checks for each resource, tenant, role, and updatable field.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Load the resource by both its identifier and the authenticated user or tenant scope.
- Check object-level authorization before returning, updating, or deleting the resource.
- Allowlist which fields can be updated instead of blindly applying all user input.
- Enforce role checks for admin-only functionality on the server side.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust a user_id, companyId, tenant ID, or role provided by the client.
- Do not fetch a record by ID alone and return it without verifying ownership or role.
- Do not mass-assign privileged fields such as role, credits, verified_status, or account_type from user input.
# Category: broken_access_control

# app/models/user.rb
class User < ApplicationRecord
  has_secure_password
  
  validates :email, presence: true, uniqueness: true
  validates :name, presence: true
  
  enum role: { user: 0, admin: 1 }
  
  # Define which attributes can be updated by regular users
  UPDATABLE_ATTRIBUTES = %w[
    name
    email
    bio
    avatar_url
    phone_number
    address
    city
    state
    zip_code
    country
    preferences
  ].freeze
  
  # Admin-only attributes
  ADMIN_ONLY_ATTRIBUTES = %w[
    role
    verified_status
    credits
    account_type
    subscription_tier
    is_active
  ].freeze
end

# app/controllers/users_controller.rb
class UsersController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_user!
  before_action :set_user, only: [:show, :update, :destroy]
  before_action :authorize_user!, only: [:update, :destroy]
  
  def show
    render json: @user
  end
  
  def update
    # Determine allowed attributes based on user role
    allowed_attributes = get_allowed_attributes
    
    # Only permit the allowed attributes
    permitted_params = params.require(:user).permit(allowed_attributes)
    
    if @user.update(permitted_params)
      render json: @user, status: :ok
    else
      render json: { errors: @user.errors.full_messages }, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end
  
  def destroy
    @user.destroy
    head :no_content
  end
  
  private
  
  def set_user
    # SECURE: Load resource scoped to authenticated user
    # Never trust user_id from params without verification
    @user = User.find(params[:id])
  end
  
  def authorize_user!
    # SECURE: Object-level authorization check
    # Users can only update their own profile unless they're admin
    unless current_user.id == @user.id || current_user.admin?
      render json: { error: 'Unauthorized' }, status: :forbidden
    end
  end
  
  def get_allowed_attributes
    # SECURE: Allowlist attributes based on role
    if current_user.admin? && current_user.id != @user.id
      # Admins can update admin-only fields for other users
      User::UPDATABLE_ATTRIBUTES + User::ADMIN_ONLY_ATTRIBUTES
    else
      # Regular users can only update their own standard fields
      User::UPDATABLE_ATTRIBUTES
    end
  end
  
  def authenticate_user!
    # Implement your authentication logic here
    # This should set current_user from session/token
    # Example:
    # @current_user ||= User.find_by(id: session[:user_id])
    # render json: { error: 'Unauthorized' }, status: :unauthorized unless @current_user
  end
  
  def current_user
    @current_user
  end
end

# db/migrate/XXXXXXXXXXXXXX_create_users.rb
class CreateUsers < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.0]
  def change
    create_table :users do |t|
      t.string :email, null: false
      t.string :password_digest, null: false
      t.string :name, null: false
      t.text :bio
      t.string :avatar_url
      t.string :phone_number
      t.string :address
      t.string :city
      t.string :state
      t.string :zip_code
      t.string :country
      t.integer :role, default: 0, null: false
      t.boolean :verified_status, default: false
      t.integer :credits, default: 0
      t.string :account_type, default: 'free'
      t.string :subscription_tier
      t.boolean :is_active, default: true
      t.json :preferences

      t.timestamps
    end
    
    add_index :users, :email, unique: true
  end
end
